title: Tender Smoky Chinese Pork Baozi
description: A herbal and pillowy Chinese dish featuring ghee and smoke-hot flavors, perfect for a date night, this low-calorie-friendly baozi is a delicious and savory treat. It combines the tender texture of pork with the comfort of a baozi.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large pan, heat the ghee over medium-high heat and add the pork shoulder, cooking until browned on all sides.
Add the smoked paprika, Chinese five-spice powder, salt, and black pepper to the pan and stir to combine.
Add the dates and green onions to the pan and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Allow the pork mixture to cool to room temperature.
Roll out the baozi dough and cut out small circles.
Place a tablespoon of the pork mixture in the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough over the filling and press the edges together to seal the baozi.
Brush the tops of the baozi with sesame oil and bake for 15 minutes.
Serve the baozi hot and enjoy.
